zh.Witchingham,Norfolk,England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可維其加姆
zh.Witchingham, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可維其加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William De Witchingham Knight於1328年出生在英國諾福爾可維其加姆時，他的父親Richard Roger Wychingham20歲，母親Margaret Antingham18歲。他和Margaret Braose至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1381年3月享年53歲。

zh-hans.Witchingham, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可維其加姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William De Witchingham Knight于1328年出生在英国諾福爾可維其加姆时，他的父亲Richard Roger Wychingham20岁，母亲Margaret Antingham18岁。他和Margaret Braose至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1381年3月享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Witchingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Kitchenham
meanings: English: locative name from Kitchingham (in Etchingham, Sussex) or Kitchenham (in Peasmarsh, Sussex).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William De Witchingham Knight was born in 1328, in Witchingham, Norfolk, England, his father, Richard Roger Wychingham, was 20 and his mother, Margaret Antingham, was 18. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Margaret Braose. He died in March 1381, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William De Witchingham Knight est né en 1328, à Witchingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Richard Roger Wychingham, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margaret Antingham, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Margaret Braose. Il est décédé en mars 1381, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William De Witchingham Knight 1328 in Witchingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Roger Wychingham 20 und seine Mutter Margaret Antingham 18. Er hatte mit Margaret Braose mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb im März 1381 im Alter von 53 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De Witchingham Knight è nato il nel 1328 nel Witchingham, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Richard Roger Wychingham, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Antingham, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Margaret Braose. Morì nel marzo 1381, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William De Witchingham Knightが1328年にイギリスノーフォルクウィトチンガムで生まれた時，父Richard Roger Wychinghamは20歳，母Margaret Antinghamは18歳であった。彼はMargaret Braoseとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1381年3月に53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William De Witchingham Knight(이)가 1328년 영국 노르폴크 윗친그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Roger Wychingham(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Antingham(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Margaret Braose(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1381년 3월 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William De Witchingham Knight nasceu em 1328, em Witchingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Roger Wychingham, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Antingham, tinha 18 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Margaret Braose. Ele faleceu em março de 1381, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William De Witchingham Knight родился в 1328 г. в местности вичингхам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Roger Wychingham было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Antingham, 18. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Margaret Braose. Он умер марта 1381 г. в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William De Witchingham Knight nació en 1328, en Witchingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Roger Wychingham, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margaret Antingham, tenía 18 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Margaret Braose. Murió en marzo de 1381, a la edad de 53 años.
